description_long: This is a large, four-engine propeller-driven airplane with a streamlined fuselage and a high-mounted tail. The aircraft features a blue and white livery with a yellow stripe running along the fuselage and a prominent yellow lion emblem on the vertical stabilizer. The wings are straight and mounted low on the fuselage, each supporting two turboprop engines with visible propellers. The cockpit is located at the front of the fuselage, with multiple windows providing visibility for the crew. The landing gear is not visible, suggesting it may be retractable. The overall design is reminiscent of classic mid-20th-century transport or passenger aircraft.
description: A large, four-engine propeller airplane with blue, white, and yellow livery and a lion emblem on the tail. It features a streamlined fuselage, low-mounted straight wings, and a forward cockpit.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the fuselage, four engines, and the lion emblem on the tail.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the nose, cockpit windows, and the symmetrical arrangement of the four propeller engines.
description_view_2: Opposite side view mirroring view 0, with clear visibility of the engines and tail design.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the tail structure, horizontal stabilizers, and the alignment of the engines and wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Low-wing configuration with straight wings and no visible winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Four turboprop engines mounted on the wings, two per side.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Landing gear not visible; likely retractable tricycle configuration based on typical design.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, enclosed cockpit with multiple windows, likely multi-seat.
